expectations.text: HealthComponent as a separate Node child emitting typed signals health_changed(new_health: int, max_health: int) and died
expectations.evidence: HealthComponent extends Node with 'signal health_changed(new_health: int, max_health: int)' and 'signal died'.

expectations.text: StateMachine node owning State child nodes wired via @export, warning against a match block in _process
expectations.evidence: StateMachine owns Idle/Run/Air/Dash State children; 'each state @exports the player... Wire in the editor — no hardcoded state names'; explicitly 'not a match block in _process'.

expectations.text: Abilities as child Nodes extending a base Ability class with a _execute() hook and cooldown ticking in _process
expectations.evidence: FireballAbility extends Node directly with _cast() and a child Cooldown Timer — no base Ability class, no _execute() hook, cooldown via Timer not _process.

expectations.text: Signals connected in _ready using Callable syntax, not in _process
expectations.evidence: _ready: 'health_component.died.connect(_on_health_died)' and 'health_component.health_changed.connect(...)'.

expectations.text: Avoids get_node path strings; uses @export references or signals
expectations.evidence: 'Every cross-node reference is an editor-wired @export or a signal, never a get_node("../..") path.'

expectations.text: HealthComponent as a separate Node child emitting typed signals health_changed and died
expectations.evidence: HealthComponent with 'signal health_changed(current: int, maximum: int)' and 'signal died'.

expectations.text: StateMachine node owning State child nodes wired via @export, warning against a match block in _process
expectations.evidence: States auto-collected from get_children() into a Dictionary and switched via string keys (transition_to(&"Air")); not @export-wired, and no warning against match in _process.

expectations.text: Abilities as child Nodes extending a base Ability class with a _execute() hook and cooldown ticking in _process
expectations.evidence: Uses a base Ability class but with _perform() (not _execute()) and a Timer-based cooldown (not _process ticking).

expectations.text: Signals connected in _ready using Callable syntax, not in _process
expectations.evidence: _ready: 'health.died.connect(_on_died)'.

expectations.text: Avoids get_node path strings; uses @export references or signals
expectations.evidence: HUD uses 'get_tree().get_first_node_in_group("player").get_node("HealthComponent")' — runtime path/group lookup rather than @export injection.

expectations.text: Object Pool as pre-instantiated nodes toggled with visible=false and process_mode=PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED
expectations.evidence: deactivate(): hide() + 'process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED'; activate() re-enables. Pre-instantiated in _ready.

expectations.text: Flyweight as a custom Resource subclass with @export fields, same .tres shared across bullets
expectations.evidence: 'BulletType extends Resource' with @export damage/speed/texture; 'assign the same .tres file to all bullets of that type.'

expectations.text: Warns modifying a shared Resource changes all holders; duplicate() for per-instance
expectations.evidence: 'never mutate a shared resource at runtime... If you genuinely need a mutated copy, stats.duplicate().'

expectations.text: Pool resets bullet state when retrieving from the pool
expectations.evidence: 'Reset every field in activate(). The #1 pool bug is a recycled bullet keeping the previous shot's position/velocity/lifetime.'

expectations.text: Uses a typed Resource subclass, not a Dictionary or plain class for shared stats
expectations.evidence: BulletType Resource used throughout; no Dictionary. Also upgraded to an O(1) free-list over the skill's O(n) scan.

expectations.text: Object Pool as pre-instantiated nodes toggled with visible=false and process_mode=PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED
expectations.evidence: Toggles with hide() + set_physics_process(false) + set_deferred("monitoring", false); does not use process_mode = PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED (different mechanism).

expectations.text: Flyweight as a custom Resource subclass with @export fields, same .tres shared across bullets
expectations.evidence: 'BulletType extends Resource' with @export; 'hand the same reference to every bullet of that type.'

expectations.text: Warns modifying a shared Resource changes all holders; duplicate() for per-instance
expectations.evidence: 'Never mutate a shared resource at runtime... duplicate() the resource... Don't set resource_local_to_scene = true.'

expectations.text: Pool resets bullet state when retrieving from the pool
expectations.evidence: activate(pos, dir, stats) resets position, velocity, texture on retrieval.

expectations.text: Uses a typed Resource subclass, not a Dictionary or plain class for shared stats
expectations.evidence: BulletType Resource with preload'd .tres; no Dictionary.

expectations.text: Requires an is_instance_valid(self) guard after every await
expectations.evidence: Guards after animation_finished and after the timer await: 'the node can be freed externally (scene change, level reset) while the coroutine is suspended.'

expectations.text: Uses await get_tree().create_timer(0.5).timeout for the wait
expectations.evidence: 'await get_tree().create_timer(0.5).timeout before queue_free().'

expectations.text: Timeout pattern uses a responded flag set by CONNECT_ONE_SHOT on both signals, polled with await get_tree().process_frame
expectations.evidence: pressed/timed_out flags via CONNECT_ONE_SHOT on confirmed and the timer; 'while not pressed and not timed_out: await get_tree().process_frame'.

expectations.text: Notes Godot has no Promise.race equivalent
expectations.evidence: 'Godot has no Promise.race, so you can't await two awaitables and take whichever fires first.'

expectations.text: Includes is_instance_valid(self) inside the polling loop
expectations.evidence: Inside the while loop: 'if not is_instance_valid(self): return false'.

expectations.text: Requires an is_instance_valid(self) guard after every await
expectations.evidence: Death-sequence code omits per-await self guards; explicitly argues 'Your final queue_free() is last, so it's safe here' and only guards references to other nodes.

expectations.text: Uses await get_tree().create_timer(0.5).timeout for the wait
expectations.evidence: 'await get_tree().create_timer(0.5).timeout' then queue_free().

expectations.text: Timeout pattern uses a responded flag set by CONNECT_ONE_SHOT on both signals, polled with await get_tree().process_frame
expectations.evidence: Button variant: state.done flag with CONNECT_ONE_SHOT on button.pressed and timer.timeout; 'while not state.done: await get_tree().process_frame'.

expectations.text: Notes Godot has no Promise.race equivalent
expectations.evidence: 'There is no built-in await first of N signals, so you either poll a frame signal or race two real signals.'

expectations.text: Includes is_instance_valid(self) inside the polling loop
expectations.evidence: Neither timeout variant checks is_instance_valid inside the while loop.

notes: Baseline answers were high quality (correct, idiomatic Godot) but consistently missed the skill's specific idioms: @export-wired state nodes vs string-keyed transitions, process_mode=PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED pooling, and is_instance_valid(self) after every await + inside poll loops.
Most discriminating: coroutine safety (S3) and the player-scene wiring conventions (S1). Bullet pool (S2) least discriminating — baseline knows pooling + Resource flyweight well.
